seed_instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same?
org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon.
new_constraint: Write the email in a highly formal tone with complex sentence structures and academic terminology.
instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same? The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon. Write the email in a highly formal tone with complex sentence structures and academic terminology.
response:
Please note that your request presents a stylistic contradiction: you have asked for an email that is **easy to read and avoids jargon**, yet simultaneously requested it be written in a **highly formal tone with complex sentence structures and academic terminology.**

These elements are inherently opposed. A highly formal, academic tone with complex sentences is, by definition, *not* easy to read for a general audience.

I will provide you with **two options** to allow you to choose the flavor that best suits your intent:

1.  **Option A: Highly Formal and Academic (Fulfilling the complex language request)**
2.  **Option B: Formal yet Accessible (Fulfilling the "easy to read" request while maintaining politeness)**

***

## Option A: Highly Formal and Academic (Complex & Jargon-Heavy)

*This option meets the formal/academic criteria but will be dense and challenging to read.*

**Subject: Solicitation for Collaborative Intellectual Discourse: The Establishment of a Literary Salon**

Esteemed colleagues and intellectual companions,

It is with considerable anticipation and profound scholarly eagerness that I extend this missive to initiate the formal constitution of a dedicated literary consortium. Having observed a discernible convergence of shared literary proclivities amongst our acquaintance, I propose the commencement of a structured, periodic colloquium wherein we might undertake a rigorous and multifaceted examination of selected published works.

The inherent value of such a gathering resides not merely in the passive reception of narratives, but rather in the dialectical process—the vigorous, yet collegial, interrogation of thematic undercurrents, narrative methodologies, and the philosophical implications embedded within the textual corpus.

To facilitate the procedural logistics, I propose an initial convening date on [Insert Date] at [Insert Time] at [Insert Location/Virtual Link]. Furthermore, I respectfully solicit your preliminary inclinations regarding the genre or specific literary period that might serve as our inaugural focus for scrutiny.

Kindly signify your assent or any reservations pertaining to this intellectual endeavor by [Insert RSVP Date], enabling the meticulous orchestration of subsequent logistical arrangements. I eagerly await the possibility of engaging in such a stimulating exchange of erudition.

With the utmost respect,

[Your Name]

***

## Option B: Formal yet Accessible (Polite, Structured, but Easy to Read)

*This option is formal and sophisticated without resorting to unnecessarily dense academic language, making it much easier for friends to read and engage with.*

**Subject: Invitation to Form a Book Club: A Shared Literary Journey**

Dear Friends,

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to propose the establishment of a dedicated book club among our circle of friends. I have long appreciated our thoughtful conversations, and I believe that dedicating time to explore literature together would be a wonderful way to deepen our connections and spark stimulating discussions.

The purpose of this club would be simple: to select a book, read it individually, and then gather to discuss its themes, characters, and overall impact. We can keep the atmosphere relaxed while still encouraging deep and meaningful conversation.

To get started, I would like to suggest meeting on [Insert Day of the Week] at [Insert Time] at [Insert Location].

Please let me know if this schedule works for you, and perhaps suggest a genre or title you would be interested in starting with!

I sincerely hope you will join me in this literary venture.

Warmest regards,

[Your Name]<turn|>
